name: Fake Chick-Fil-a-Chicken Sandwich
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the peanut oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat to about 400°F.
2. In a small bowl, beat the egg and stir in the milk.
3. In a separate bowl, combine the flour, powdered sugar, pepper, and salt.
4. Dip each piece of chicken in the milk until it is fully moistened.
5. Roll the moistened chicken in the flour mixture until completely coated.
6. Drop all four chicken pieces into the hot oil and close the pressure cooker lid.
7. When steam starts shooting through the pressure release valve, set the timer for 3-1/2 minutes.
8. While the chicken is cooking, spread a coating of melted butter on the top of each bun.
9. When the chicken is done, remove it from the oil and drain or blot on paper towels.
10. Place two pickles on each bottom bun; add a chicken breast, then the top bun.
11. To make a deluxe chicken sandwich, simply add two tomato slices and a leaf of lettuce.
12. Mayonnaise also goes well on this sandwich—it is served as a side at the restaurant.
